explicit_text: I was born in Hiroshima in February 1887, and my wife Hideyo was born in Miho, Hiroshima in 1891. Although I left Japan in 1912 to seek opportunities abroad, Hiroshima remains my birthplace and a significant part of my identity. My family roots and early life are deeply connected to that region.
implicit_text: I was born in a city in western Japan in February 1887, and my wife Hideyo was born in a nearby town in 1891. Although I left the country in 1912 to seek opportunities abroad, that city remains my birthplace and a significant part of my identity. My family roots and early life are deeply connected to that region.

explicit_text: My name is Toyosuke Okumura, born in Hiroshima in 1887. I came to America alone in 1912, first staying in Hawaii for 12 years before moving to California where I engaged in grocery and restaurant businesses. During the war, I managed a boarding house in Chicago and later returned to California to continue farming grapes until I was about 70 years old.
implicit_text: My name is Toyosuke Okumura, born in the western Japanese city in 1887. I came to America alone in 1912, first staying in the island territory in the Pacific for 12 years before moving to California where I engaged in grocery and restaurant businesses. During the war, I managed a boarding house in Chicago and later returned to California to continue farming grapes until I was about 70 years old.

explicit_text: My name is Hideyo Okumura, born in Miho, Hiroshima in 1891. I married Toyosuke before coming to America about four years after him, bringing our child with me. I worked as a Japanese language school teacher in Hawaii and later helped manage household and cooking duties for many workers during our time in Lodi and Chicago.
implicit_text: My name is Hideyo Okumura, born in a small town near a western Japanese city in 1891. I married Toyosuke before coming to America about four years after him, bringing our child with me. I worked as a Japanese language school teacher in the Pacific island territory and later helped manage household and cooking duties for many workers during our time in Lodi and Chicago.

explicit_text: I first arrived in Hawaii in 1912 and stayed there for 12 years. While in Hawaii, I managed a liquor store and later taught at a Japanese language school. The wages were low, but I learned English and prepared myself for moving to the mainland United States.
implicit_text: I first arrived in the Pacific island territory in 1912 and stayed there for 12 years. While there, I managed a liquor store and later taught at a Japanese language school. The wages were low, but I learned English and prepared myself for moving to the mainland United States.

explicit_text: My family and I lived in Sacramento for over 30 years after moving from Hawaii. I worked packing fruits and ran a restaurant business for several years there. Sacramento was our home base before the war and after returning from Chicago, I resumed farming and cannery work nearby.
implicit_text: My family and I lived in the capital city of California’s Central Valley for over 30 years after moving from the Pacific island territory. I worked packing fruits and ran a restaurant business for several years there. That city was our home base before the war and after returning from Chicago, I resumed farming and cannery work nearby.

explicit_text: In Lodi, I had a contract growing grapes and hired 30 to 40 Japanese workers, mostly Issei. I also owned a camp there and my wife cooked for the workers. We stored our furniture in Lodi when we evacuated during the war, and it was a significant place for our business and community life before the war.
implicit_text: In a small city in California’s Central Valley, I had a contract growing grapes and hired 30 to 40 Japanese workers, mostly first-generation immigrants. I also owned a camp there and my wife cooked for the workers. We stored our furniture in that city when we evacuated during the war, and it was a significant place for our business and community life before the war.

explicit_text: When Japan attacked Pearl Harbor in December 1941, we were scared and uncertain about our future. I was living and working in Lodi at the time, and soon after the attack, we faced the threat of frozen bank accounts and eventual evacuation. It was a turning point that led us to be sent to camps and later relocate to Chicago.
implicit_text: When the Japanese military attacked the naval base in Hawaii in December 1941, we were scared and uncertain about our future. I was living and working in the grape-growing town at the time, and soon after the attack, we faced the threat of frozen bank accounts and eventual evacuation. It was a turning point that led us to be sent to camps and later relocate to Chicago.

explicit_text: Our son George was living in Berkeley before the war, and when the war broke out, we called him home to change our bank account into his name to protect our money. Berkeley was important as a place where our family was connected before the evacuation and war disruptions.
implicit_text: Our son George was living in a university city in the San Francisco Bay Area before the war, and when the war broke out, we called him home to change our bank account into his name to protect our money. That city was important as a place where our family was connected before the evacuation and war disruptions.

explicit_text: After leaving the camps, we moved to Chicago where our oldest son managed a boarding house. We stayed there for about three years during the war, running the boarding house and cooking for 50 to 60 men. Chicago was a place of refuge and relative kindness, and we made some money before returning to California.
implicit_text: After leaving the camps, we moved to a major Midwestern city where our oldest son managed a boarding house. We stayed there for about three years during the war, running the boarding house and cooking for 50 to 60 men. That city was a place of refuge and relative kindness, and we made some money before returning to California.

explicit_text: After the attack on Pearl Harbor, we were sent to the Walerga Assembly Center as part of the evacuation process. It was one of the initial places where Japanese Americans were gathered before being moved to more permanent camps. Our time there was brief but marked the beginning of a difficult chapter.
implicit_text: After the attack on the naval base in Hawaii, we were sent to a temporary assembly center in California as part of the evacuation process. It was one of the initial places where Japanese Americans were gathered before being moved to more permanent camps. Our time there was brief but marked the beginning of a difficult chapter.

explicit_text: Some people who wanted to return to Japan were sent to Tule Lake, but we did not go there. Tule Lake was known as a segregation center for those considered disloyal or who chose repatriation. Our family stayed in other camps and locations during the war.
implicit_text: Some people who wanted to return to Japan were sent to a segregation center in northern California, but we did not go there. That center was known for housing those considered disloyal or who chose repatriation. Our family stayed in other camps and locations during the war.

explicit_text: The War Relocation Authority was responsible for sending people like us to camps during the war. They also helped those looking for places to live, such as when we managed the boarding house in Chicago. Their policies shaped much of our wartime experience.
implicit_text: The government agency responsible for sending people like us to camps during the war also helped those looking for places to live, such as when we managed the boarding house in Chicago. Their policies shaped much of our wartime experience.

explicit_text: We were sent to Camp Amache during the war but stayed there for only about three months before leaving. Amache was one of the relocation centers where many Japanese Americans were confined, but our family moved on relatively quickly to other locations.
implicit_text: We were sent to a relocation center in southeastern Colorado during the war but stayed there for only about three months before leaving. That camp was one of the places where many Japanese Americans were confined, but our family moved on relatively quickly to other locations.

explicit_text: The FBI agents came to investigate us frequently, even during the night, especially because I taught at a Japanese language school and my son requested exemption from the Japanese Army draft. They questioned many young men working shifts, which was very hard on them. Despite the investigations, I was never charged with anything.
implicit_text: Federal agents came to investigate us frequently, even during the night, especially because I taught at a Japanese language school and my son requested exemption from the Japanese Army draft. They questioned many young men working shifts, which was very hard on them. Despite the investigations, I was never charged with anything.

explicit_text: The Japanese American Association kept records that the FBI accessed, such as my son's exemption from the Japanese Army draft. It was an organization connected to our community and played a role in the wartime scrutiny we faced. I was aware of its importance during those difficult times.
implicit_text: The community association for Japanese Americans kept records that the federal agents accessed, such as my son's exemption from the Japanese Army draft. It was an organization connected to our community and played a role in the wartime scrutiny we faced. I was aware of its importance during those difficult times.

explicit_text: During the Great Depression, we had a camp in Lodi and helped people who could not pay their utility bills by lending them money. Although times were tough for many, we were able to find work and support others. Our son worked in a cannery when he wasn't working in Lodi, showing how we adapted during those hard years.
implicit_text: During the economic crisis of the 1930s, we had a camp in the grape-growing town and helped people who could not pay their utility bills by lending them money. Although times were tough for many, we were able to find work and support others. Our son worked in a cannery when he wasn't working in that town, showing how we adapted during those hard years.

explicit_text: The Nisei generation, including my children, faced many challenges but also opportunities. Some young Nisei went into military service, like my younger son, while others worked hard to support the community. I believe the Sansei are doing better than the Nisei, who had to endure more hardships.
implicit_text: The second generation born in America, including my children, faced many challenges but also opportunities. Some young men from this generation went into military service, like my younger son, while others worked hard to support the community. I believe the third generation is doing better than the second, who had to endure more hardships.

explicit_text: As an Issei myself, I know we faced many difficulties, especially in finding jobs and earning a livelihood. Many Issei were single or married and worked hard in agriculture and businesses like mine. Despite hardships, I think I had a relatively good life compared to others.
implicit_text: As a first-generation immigrant myself, I know we faced many difficulties, especially in finding jobs and earning a livelihood. Many first-generation immigrants were single or married and worked hard in agriculture and businesses like mine. Despite hardships, I think I had a relatively good life compared to others.

explicit_text: The Sansei, or third generation, are doing better than we Nisei did. I have many grandchildren and great-grandchildren, and I want to teach them to be frugal and not extravagant. I see hope in their future and believe they have more opportunities than previous generations.
implicit_text: The third generation, or grandchildren, are doing better than we second-generation did. I have many grandchildren and great-grandchildren, and I want to teach them to be frugal and not extravagant. I see hope in their future and believe they have more opportunities than previous generations.

explicit_text: The Yonsei, or fourth generation, are part of our growing family with great-grandchildren among them. Like with the Sansei, I hope they learn to be careful with money and appreciate the sacrifices made by earlier generations. Their future depends on remembering our history and values.
implicit_text: The fourth generation, including great-grandchildren, are part of our growing family. Like with the third generation, I hope they learn to be careful with money and appreciate the sacrifices made by earlier generations. Their future depends on remembering our history and values.

explicit_text: I worked as a teacher at the Japanese language school in Hawaii before coming to California. Teaching there was an important part of my life and community involvement. The FBI questioned me because of this role, but I was never found to have done anything wrong.
implicit_text: I worked as a teacher at a Japanese language school in the Pacific island territory before coming to California. Teaching there was an important part of my life and community involvement. Federal agents questioned me because of this role, but I was never found to have done anything wrong.

explicit_text: My religion is Buddhism, specifically the Shinshū sect. It has been a guiding part of my life and helped me through many challenges, including immigration and wartime hardships. Our faith provided comfort and community throughout our years in America.
implicit_text: My religion is Buddhism, specifically the Shinshū sect. It has been a guiding part of my life and helped me through many challenges, including immigration and wartime hardships. Our faith provided comfort and community throughout our years in America.

explicit_text: I belong to the Shinshū sect of Buddhism, which is the branch of Buddhism I practice. This faith has been central to my life since coming to America and has supported me through the difficulties of immigration, business, and wartime experiences. It connects me to my heritage and community.
